items.content: Vistra (VST) stock surged approximately 8% in premarket trading after Meta Platforms signed 20-year agreements to purchase nuclear power from three Vistra plants in Ohio and Pennsylvania. These deals aim to secure reliable, zero-carbon electricity for Meta's surging data center demand and AI buildout. The agreements involve over 2,600 megawatts of energy, including existing generation and output increases, with Meta's purchases starting in late 2026.

items.content: The First Trust Dow Jones Internet Index Fund (FDN) offers an equal-weight alternative to the Nasdaq and QQQ by focusing on pure internet companies. However, FDN has significantly underperformed QQQ over the past one and five years, primarily due to its lack of exposure to mega-cap tech stocks like Nvidia, Apple, and Microsoft. items.content: Vistra (NYSE: VST) stock surged over 13% in premarket trading after announcing a 20-year nuclear energy agreement with Meta. items.title: Why Constellation Energy Stock Popped Today
items.content: Constellation Energy (CEG) stock rose by 4% due to two positive news items. Investment bank TD Cowen initiated coverage with a buy rating and a $440 price target, and Meta Platforms announced an expanded commitment to nuclear energy, indirectly securing Constellation's future revenue. Despite the positive news, the stock is considered expensive at 37 times trailing earnings compared to its 15% long-term earnings growth forecast.

items.content: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) has transformed into a leading challenger in the AI semiconductor market, largely due to its strategic shift towards high-performance computing and data center AI under CEO Lisa Su. items.title: Here's What the Street Thinks About Amazon.com (AMZN)
items.content: Wolfe Research recently lowered Amazon's (AMZN) price target to $275 from $305, while Evercore ISI maintained a Buy rating with a $335 target, citing the potential of its AI-powered shopping assistant, Rufus. Evercore anticipates Rufus and agentic commerce could significantly boost Amazon's advertising revenue and retail GMV by 2028. The article suggests that while Amazon has investment potential, other AI stocks might offer greater upside and less risk.

items.title: Kenvue Appoints Madhav Nayak as Vice President and CMO for Asia-Pacific
items.content: Kenvue has appointed Madhav Nayak as Vice President and Chief Marketing Officer for Asia-Pacific. He will lead brand strategy and commercial performance for Kenvue's brands across the region, including Johnson's, Neutrogena, and Listerine. Nayak brings over two decades of FMCG and consumer marketing expertise, having previously held leadership roles at KFC Asia, Meta, and Unilever.

items.title: Break market concentration with global stocks
items.content: This article argues for diversifying portfolios with global stocks to mitigate the risks associated with market concentration in a few large US tech and AI firms. It highlights that the "AI trade" is becoming more nuanced, requiring careful selection of companies embedding AI meaningfully, rather than just experimenting. The authors advocate for an active, fundamentals-driven investment approach to capture global opportunities and ensure more resilient portfolios.

items.title: Why Constellation Energy Rallied Nearly 60% in 2025
items.content: Constellation Energy's shares rallied nearly 60% in 2025 due to its position as a key player in the AI buildout and increasing electricity demand. The company announced a major acquisition of Calpine, adding significant natural gas and geothermal capacity to its nuclear fleet. Additionally, Constellation secured new power purchase agreements with AI hyperscalers and benefited from a more favorable regulatory environment.

items.content: Jim Cramer is optimistic about Amazon's performance in 2026, citing strong growth in its web services business, significant investment in AI through Anthropic, and continued double-digit growth in its retail sector. items.title: The Truth About International Business Machines: Why Everyone Is Suddenly Paying Attention
items.content: This article examines International Business Machines (IBM) as a potential AI dark horse, arguing that while it lacks the social media hype of other tech giants, its focus on enterprise AI, hybrid cloud, and automation makes it an attractive investment for those seeking long-term value and dividends. The piece contrasts IBM's "grown-up tech" stability with the high-volatility, meme-stock plays, positioning it as a steady alternative for a diversified portfolio.

items.title: Callan JMB director Dial buys shares worth $2409
items.content: Callan JMB Inc. director Gerald Dial recently purchased 2,000 shares of the company's common stock for $2,409. The purchases were made at prices between $1.20 and $1.22 per share, which is below the stock's Fair Value according to InvestingPro data. The company has also announced strategic initiatives including a preliminary agreement for manufacturing equipment at its Texas facility and an expansion into food sampling logistics.
